schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Quick Summary of the Model I Used: δ_ε(r)

theory.canonical_statement: ### Quick summary of the model I used - Two fields in 2D polar coordinates \((r,\theta)\): \(\psi_1(r,\theta)\propto r^{\ell} e^{i\ell\theta} e^{-r^2/(2\sigma^2)}\) (your vortex / toroidal field, winding exponent \(\ell\)). \(\psi_2(r,\theta)\propto e^{-r^2/(2\sigma^2)}\) (a symmetric probe field). - Soft delta approximated by a normalized 2-D Gaussian: \(\delta_\epsilon(r)=(2\pi\epsilon^2)^{-1}e^{-r^2/(2\epsilon^2)}\). - Overlap integral computed numerically: \[ I(\ell)=\int_0^{2\pi}\!\!\int_0^\infty \overline{\psi_1}\,\delta_\epsilon(r)\,\psi_2 \; r\,dr\,d\theta . \] - Important physics note used analytically: with a perfectly angularly symmetric \(\psi_2\) the angular integral \(\int_0^{2\pi} e^{-i\ell\theta}d\theta\) vanishes for any nonzero \(\ell\). That means a perfectly symmetric probe (and true δ at the origin) only samples the \(\ell=0\) (non-vortex) component — vortex modes with nonzero integer or fractional charge have a node at \(r=0\) and give zero direct overlap. - To make the problem physically interesting (and get nonzero overlaps for \(\ell\ne0\)), I introduced a small angular asymmetry in \(\psi_2\): \(\psi_2\to \psi_2(1+\alpha\cos\theta)\) with \(\alpha=0.15\). This models e.g. imperfect alignment, a probe with a slight dipole moment, or a measurement device that breaks perfect rotational symmetry — realistic in experiments.
theory.technical_interpretation: Quick Summary of the Model I Used: δ_ε(r) is an exponential / gaussian profile, written δ_ε(r)=(2πε^2)^-1e^-r^2/(2ε^2). It describes an exponential or Gaussian profile characterizing localized or decaying behaviour. It is built from π, ε, r, defining δ_ε(r). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where soft delta approximated by a normalized 2-D Gaussian
